channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Bank of Tanzania pushes for digital payments to improve cross-border trade
item.description: Dar es Salaam – The Deputy Governor of Bank of Tanzania (BoT), Sauda Msemo, has urged traders and stakeholders to quickly adopt digital payment systems in cross-border trade. She pointed out that whil...

item.title: Electrical Spark Causes Temporary Shutdown Of MMIA Terminal 3 Operations
item.description: The Federal Airports Authority of Nigeria (FAAN) talk say small electrical spark for Murtala Muhammed International Airport (MMIA) Terminal 3 cause temporary shutdown of operations on Wednesday. Accor...

item.title: Trump Appears in Supreme Court for Hearing on Birthright Citizenship Executive Order
item.description: President Donald Trump attended a Supreme Court hearing on Wednesday concerning the legality of his executive order aimed at ending birthright citizenship for children born in the United States to par...

item.title: Nestory Irankunda stars as Socceroos defeat Curaçao 5-1 in final home match before World Cup
item.description: In their last home match ahead of the FIFA World Cup in June, the Socceroos secured a convincing 5-1 victory over Curaçao in Melbourne. Young Australian forward Nestory Irankunda, 20, made a remarkabl...

item.title: Who actually decides when Australia sends troops to war?
item.description: Australia’s power to commit troops to war rests with the executive branch of government, not with parliament. The Constitution makes the governor‑general commander‑in‑chief of the Australian Defence F...

item.title: Thirty Years After Halifax Amalgamation: Mixed Views on Its Impact
item.description: Thirty years after the forced amalgamation that created the Halifax Regional Municipality (HRM) on April 1, 1996, opinions on its legacy remain divided. The merger joined Halifax, Dartmouth, Bedford, ...

item.title: Family Dispute and Corporate Secrecy in the Lopez Holdings and Razon Energy Ventures
item.description: The Lopez family, a long‑standing Philippine business clan, is currently facing an internal conflict between cousins Federico “Piki” Lopez and Eugenio “Gabby” Lopez III. The issue centers on major ene...

item.title: Seattle man narrowly escapes being pushed onto tracks in attempted murder at Northgate train station
item.description: A man named Peter Michael Walbrun narrowly survived an attempted murder at Seattle’s Northgate train station on 19 March 2026. While standing on the platform and checking his phone, Walbrun was sudden...

item.title: Presidential aspirants discuss strategies to strengthen national unity and political stability
item.description: Dar es Salaam – President Samia Suluhu Hassan met with leaders from 16 political parties who contested in the 2026 General Elections. The meeting aimed at discussing the political situation and nation...

item.title: Ethical Questions Arise as New York Times Critic Uses AI in Book Review
item.description: In January 2026, Alex Preston, a New York Times critic, used artificial intelligence to assist in writing his review of Jean-Baptiste Andrea’s novel Watching Over Her. Preston incorporated phrases and...

item.title: IOC introduces new policy banning transgender athletes from women’s events
item.description: The International Olympic Committee (IOC) has announced a significant policy change that will prevent transgender athletes from competing in women’s categories. Under the new rule, eligibility will be...

item.title: The Comeback Season 3 explores AI in television and Valerie Cherish’s evolution
item.description: After a twelve-year hiatus, Lisa Kudrow returns as Valerie Cherish in Season 3 of The Comeback, now streaming on HBO and Crave in Canada. Unlike previous seasons, which followed Valerie navigating rea...

item.title: Inspekta Mwala Poses with Retired Vitimbi Actor Likobe at Funeral
item.description: Davis Mwabili, better known as Inspekta Mwala, recently shared a nostalgic photo with the legendary Vitimbi actor, Likobe, during a funeral, sparking excitement among fans. Likobe, whose real name is ...

item.title: Nine injured including four policemen in Lakki Marwat IED explosion
item.description: Nine individuals, including four policemen, sustained injuries in an improvised explosive device (IED) attack in the Serai Naurang area of Lakki Marwat district, Khyber Pakhtunkhwa, on Wednesday. Acco...

item.title: Russian First Deputy PM Denis Manturov to Hold Talks on West Asia Conflict and India-Russia Cooperation
item.description: Russian First Deputy Prime Minister Denis Manturov is scheduled to visit India for a two-day diplomatic engagement starting Thursday. During his visit, he is expected to hold discussions with National...

item.title: Dough Works Limited expansion boosts jobs and economic activity in Tanzania
item.description: Dough Works Limited (DWL), the company operating KFC and Pizza Hut in Tanzania, has created over 800 jobs as it continues to grow its presence in the local market. The firm has recently opened its 13t...

item.title: The Rise of Sleep Tourism: Destinations for Rest and Well-Being
item.description: Sleep tourism is emerging as a notable trend in wellness travel, focusing on improving sleep quality and reducing stress, burnout, and chronic fatigue. Unlike traditional holidays centred on sightseei...

item.title: Six Metropolitan Police officers investigated over injury of 16-year-old during stop and search in north London
item.description: Six Metropolitan Police officers are under investigation after a 16-year-old Black boy sustained a fractured knee during a stop and search operation in Tottenham, north London, in April 2025. Accordin...

item.title: Meet the Artemis II Crew: The Next Astronauts to Orbit the Moon
item.description: NASA is preparing to launch Artemis II, a historic mission marking the first crewed lunar orbit in over 50 years. Scheduled for April 1, 2026, the mission will last approximately ten days and aims to ...

item.title: Bank of Tanzania delays gold sale due to falling global prices
item.description: The Bank of Tanzania (BoT) has decided to hold back its planned gold sales after noticing a dip in global prices. By early March 2026, BoT had about 19.6 tonnes of gold, almost reaching its 20-tonne g...
